name: Windows Telemetry Remover database
description: Declarative checks and disable actions for Windows privacy-related services, policies, tasks, recommendations, feedback, synchronization, diagnostic upload, and crash reporting.
agentInstructions: Keep every id stable, lowercase, and unique.
Use valid JSON without comments because Windows PowerShell 5.1 rejects JSON comments.
Do not place executable PowerShell in the database. Use only supported typed checks and actions.
Set exfiltration to yes, possible, or no for every item.
Set severity and severityReason for every item from an enterprise secret-protection perspective.
Set threatProofUrl, threatProofExactQuote, fixProofUrl, and fixProofExactQuote for every item.

fixProofExactQuote must prove the exact target state or command effect used by disableActions; do not use generic product documentation, policy index text, or quotes that only prove a related feature exists.

threatProofExactQuote must prove the threat itself: collection, transmission, cloud processing, synchronization, account linkage, stored sensitive material, or another concrete data-exposure mechanism; do not use quotes that only describe the fix, disabled state, or policy name.
Items should normally use one disableAction per proof; if multiple actions are grouped, threatProofExactQuote and fixProofExactQuote must clearly cover every action in the group.
Set enableActions for every item so users can see how to turn the Windows feature back on.
Review downsides before adding an item to disable-all behavior.
checkCommands are documentation for humans and agents; the engine does not execute them.
Set applicableWindowsEditions and applicabilityNote for every item; isApplicable is computed at runtime from the current Windows edition family.

items.name: Connected User Experiences and Telemetry (DiagTrack)
items.description: Collects and routes Windows diagnostic and telemetry events according to the configured diagnostic-data level.
items.severityReason: diagnostic telemetry can include device, app, and managed-environment signals
items.threatProofExactQuote: Windows diagnostic data is vital technical data
items.fixProofExactQuote: `Disabled` - The service is disabled and cannot be started by a user or application.
items.downsides: Windows diagnostics, troubleshooting, Endpoint Analytics, and some managed-device reporting can become incomplete.
checkCommands: Get-CimInstance Win32_Service -Filter "Name='DiagTrack'" | Select-Object Name, State, StartMode
items.applicabilityNote: applies where the DiagTrack service exists

items.name: Device Management WAP Push routing service
items.description: Routes Wireless Application Protocol push messages used by Windows device-management scenarios.
items.severityReason: device-management traffic can expose management state in enrolled environments
items.threatProofExactQuote: The dmwappushservice service is required on client devices
items.fixProofExactQuote: `Disabled` - The service is disabled and cannot be started by a user or application.
items.downsides: Microsoft Intune, mobile-device management, work or school management, and remote policy delivery can stop working correctly.
checkCommands: Get-CimInstance Win32_Service -Filter "Name='dmwappushservice'" | Select-Object Name, State, StartMode
items.applicabilityNote: applies where the dmwappushservice service exists; mainly relevant to managed/MDM devices

items.name: Windows Error Reporting
items.description: Collects information about application, operating-system, and hardware failures and can send reports to Microsoft.
items.severityReason: crash reports can include process, module, and failure-context details
items.threatProofExactQuote: enables users to notify Microsoft of application faults, kernel faults, unresponsive applications, and other application specific problems
items.fixProofExactQuote: The Disable-WindowsErrorReporting cmdlet disables Windows Error Reporting
items.downsides: Automatic crash reporting, online problem solutions, and some diagnostic workflows will no longer work.
checkCommands: Get-CimInstance Win32_Service -Filter "Name='WerSvc'" | Select-Object Name, State, StartMode
items.applicabilityNote: applies where Windows Error Reporting is available

items.name: Machine-wide local crash-dump collection
items.description: Windows Error Reporting LocalDumps registry keys can create local process dump files after application crashes.
items.severityReason: local dumps can stage process memory with credentials or proprietary data for later collection, upload, or support-bundle exfiltration
items.threatProofExactQuote: full user-mode dumps are collected and stored locally after a user-mode application crashes
items.fixProofExactQuote: This feature is not enabled by default. To enable and configure the feature, use the following registry values under the HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Error Reporting\LocalDumps key.
items.downsides: Local crash dumps will not be generated for debugging applications and native crashes.
items.applicabilityNote: applies where WER LocalDumps registry configuration is supported

items.name: Diagnostic-data policy is not forced to the lowest requested level
items.description: Configures the Windows diagnostic-data policy value through the documented policy path and the legacy CurrentVersion\Policies fallback path. A value of 0 requests the lowest policy level, although Home and Pro editions may enforce a higher minimum.
items.severityReason: higher diagnostic levels can send more system and usage data externally
items.threatProofExactQuote: Windows diagnostic data is vital technical data
items.fixProofExactQuote: Category Value Diagnostic data off (Security) 0
items.downsides: Some diagnostics, Insider features, managed analytics, and troubleshooting information can become unavailable. Windows edition rules may prevent true zero telemetry.
items.applicabilityNote: AllowTelemetry exists on supported Windows editions, but value 0 is only fully effective on Enterprise, Education, IoT Enterprise, and Server editions; Home and Pro can enforce a higher minimum

items.name: OneSettings downloads are not disabled
items.description: Prevents Windows from periodically connecting to the OneSettings service to download configuration settings.
items.severityReason: OneSettings is a Microsoft configuration service connection path that can reveal device configuration state and receive remote configuration
items.threatProofExactQuote: Windows will periodically attempt to connect with the OneSettings service to download configuration settings.
items.fixProofExactQuote: 1 Disabled.
items.downsides: Windows may stop receiving Microsoft-delivered configuration updates that rely on OneSettings.
items.applicabilityNote: Policy CSP support: device scope on Pro, Enterprise, Education, and IoT Enterprise for Windows 11 21H2 and later

items.name: Device name is allowed in Windows diagnostic data
items.description: Prevents Windows from sending the device name to Microsoft as part of Windows diagnostic data.
items.severityReason: device names can disclose host naming schemes, user names, department names, or internal asset identifiers
items.threatProofExactQuote: This policy allows the device name to be sent to Microsoft as part of Windows diagnostic data.
items.fixProofExactQuote: 0 (Default) Disabled.
items.downsides: Managed diagnostic portals and support workflows may lose a direct device-name identifier.
items.applicabilityNote: Policy CSP support: device scope on Pro, Enterprise, Education, and IoT Enterprise

items.name: Find My Device is not disabled by policy
items.description: Disables Find My Device so Windows cannot register the device for Microsoft-account device location lookup.
items.severityReason: Find My Device can associate a device with account-based location lookup and recovery workflows
items.threatProofExactQuote: locate, and lock your Windows device from account.microsoft.com/devices
items.fixProofExactQuote: AllowFindMyDevice to 0 (zero)
items.downsides: You will not be able to locate, lock, or recover the device through Microsoft's Find My Device workflow if it is lost.
items.applicabilityNote: applies where the Find My Device Windows policy is supported; not applicable to Windows Server

items.name: Tailored experiences effective state
items.description: Prevents Windows from using diagnostic data to provide tailored tips, advertisements, and recommendations.
items.severityReason: recommendation personalization depends on diagnostic or usage signals
items.threatProofExactQuote: tailored experiences with relevant tips and recommendations
items.fixProofExactQuote: Turn off tailored experiences with relevant tips and recommendations
items.downsides: Personalized Windows tips and recommendations can become less relevant or disappear.
items.applicabilityNote: machine policy and current-user setting availability can vary by Windows release

items.name: Advertising ID is not disabled
items.description: Disables the Windows advertising identifier by machine policy and legacy runtime setting for applications that use it.
items.severityReason: advertising identifiers can correlate app activity across contexts
items.threatProofExactQuote: users can control whether apps can use the advertising ID for experiences across apps
items.fixProofExactQuote: If you enable this policy setting, the advertising ID is turned off. | Create a REG_DWORD registry setting named Enabled in HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AdvertisingInfo with a value of 0
items.downsides: Advertisements and app recommendations may be less personalized.
items.applicabilityNote: uses both machine policy and legacy runtime AdvertisingInfo values; the legacy value is applicable on Home

items.name: Activity history and activity upload are not disabled by policy
items.description: Disables the Windows activity feed and prevents applications from publishing or uploading user activities.
items.severityReason: activity history can expose application and workflow patterns across devices
items.threatProofExactQuote: ActivityFeed shall roam these activities across device graph
items.fixProofExactQuote: 0 Disabled. Apps/OS can't publish the activities
items.downsides: Cross-device activity, some recent-activity experiences, and parts of Windows Backup or managed activity reporting can stop working.
checkCommands: Get-ItemProperty -LiteralPath 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object EnableActivityFeed, PublishUserActivities, UploadUserActivities
items.applicabilityNote: Policy CSP support: device scope on Pro, Enterprise, Education, and IoT Enterprise

items.name: Cloud Clipboard synchronization is not disabled by policy
items.description: Disables cross-device clipboard synchronization so copied clipboard items cannot roam through Microsoft's cloud clipboard service.
items.severityReason: clipboard content can contain credentials, secrets, tokens, document fragments, or proprietary data and can be uploaded to the cloud when roaming is allowed
items.threatProofExactQuote: an item copied to the clipboard is uploaded to the cloud
items.fixProofExactQuote: 0 – Not allowed
items.downsides: Clipboard synchronization across Windows devices stops working. Local clipboard copy and paste remains available.
items.applicabilityNote: ADMX policy is machine-scoped and supported on Windows 10 and later

items.name: Input personalization is not disabled by machine policy
items.description: Disables automatic input personalization by machine policy.
items.severityReason: typing and inking personalization can involve sensitive input-derived signals
items.threatProofExactQuote: Microsoft uses voice input to help improve speech services.
items.fixProofExactQuote: 0 Not allowed.
items.downsides: Typing, handwriting, and language personalization can become less accurate.
items.applicabilityNote: Policy CSP support: device scope on Pro, Enterprise, Education, and IoT Enterprise

items.name: Current-user inking, typing, and personalization collection is not restricted
items.description: Restricts current-user implicit text, ink, inking/typing recognition, and contact harvesting settings used by Windows inking and typing personalization.
items.severityReason: implicit text and ink collection can involve sensitive typed or handwritten input-derived signals
items.threatProofExactQuote: To turn off Inking & Typing data collection
items.fixProofExactQuote: Set RestrictImplicitTextCollection registry REG_DWORD setting to a value of 1 | Set RestrictImplicitInkCollection registry REG_DWORD setting to a value of 1
items.downsides: Typing, handwriting, contact-based handwriting personalization, and language personalization can become less accurate for the current user.
items.applicabilityNote: current-user Windows privacy setting; exact UI availability can vary by Windows release

items.name: Current-user feedback frequency or online speech privacy
items.description: Suppresses feedback requests and records that online speech recognition has not been accepted for the current account.
items.severityReason: online speech services can process spoken content that may contain confidential material
items.threatProofExactQuote: Microsoft uses voice input to help improve speech services.
items.fixProofExactQuote: speech services will be disabled
items.downsides: Cloud-based speech recognition, dictation, and voice features that depend on online speech services can stop working.
items.applicabilityNote: applies where current-user speech privacy settings are present

items.name: Windows content delivery, suggested apps, or Spotlight
items.description: Disables Windows content delivery, system suggestions, promoted or silently delivered apps, and Windows Spotlight for the current account.
items.severityReason: content delivery and suggestions can use profile, device, or usage context
items.threatProofExactQuote: daily images from Microsoft displayed on their desktop
items.fixProofExactQuote: Spotlight collection won't show as an option
items.downsides: Windows Spotlight images, lock-screen tips, promoted apps, setup suggestions, and some recommendation content will disappear.
checkCommands: Get-ItemProperty -LiteralPath 'HKCU:\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\ContentDeliveryManager' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object ContentDeliveryAllowed, SystemPaneSuggestionsEnabled, OemPreInstalledAppsEnabled, PreInstalledAppsEnabled, SilentInstalledAppsEnabled, RotatingLockScreenEnabled, RotatingLockScreenOverlayEnabled, SubscribedContent-*
items.applicabilityNote: current-user ContentDeliveryManager values are applicable on Home; policy-backed CloudContent values should be modeled separately

items.name: Microsoft Compatibility Appraiser scheduled task
items.description: Runs CompatTelRunner to inventory applications, drivers, and hardware for Windows compatibility and upgrade assessment.
items.severityReason: compatibility inventory can disclose installed software, drivers, and hardware, but its primary purpose is upgrade compatibility assessment
items.threatProofExactQuote: components make network connections to Microsoft services by default
items.fixProofExactQuote: The `Disable-ScheduledTask` cmdlet disables a scheduled task.
items.downsides: Windows may assess feature-update compatibility and safeguard holds less effectively. Re-enable before a major Windows version upgrade if necessary.
checkCommands: Get-ScheduledTask -TaskPath '\Microsoft\Windows\Application Experience\' -TaskName 'Microsoft Compatibility Appraiser Exp' | Select-Object TaskPath, TaskName, State, @{Name='Enabled';Expression={$_.Settings.Enabled}}
items.applicabilityNote: applies where the scheduled task exists

items.name: Customer Experience Improvement Program scheduled tasks
items.description: Consolidator and UsbCeip collect or consolidate Customer Experience Improvement Program information, including USB-device experience data.
items.severityReason: CEIP data can reveal usage and device experience information
items.threatProofExactQuote: components make network connections to Microsoft services by default
items.fixProofExactQuote: The `Disable-ScheduledTask` cmdlet disables a scheduled task.
checkCommands: Get-ScheduledTask -TaskPath '\Microsoft\Windows\Customer Experience Improvement Program\' -TaskName Consolidator,UsbCeip | Select-Object TaskPath, TaskName, State, @{Name='Enabled';Expression={$_.Settings.Enabled}}
items.applicabilityNote: applies where these scheduled tasks exist

items.name: System Initiated User Feedback scheduled tasks
items.description: DmClient tasks download and run system-initiated feedback scenarios.
items.severityReason: feedback scenarios may collect or request contextual system information
items.threatProofExactQuote: components make network connections to Microsoft services by default
items.fixProofExactQuote: The `Disable-ScheduledTask` cmdlet disables a scheduled task.
items.downsides: Automatic Windows feedback prompts and some organization-managed feedback scenarios can stop working.
checkCommands: Get-ScheduledTask -TaskPath '\Microsoft\Windows\Feedback\Siuf\' -TaskName DmClient,DmClientOnScenarioDownload | Select-Object TaskPath, TaskName, State, @{Name='Enabled';Expression={$_.Settings.Enabled}}
items.applicabilityNote: applies where these scheduled tasks exist

items.name: Microsoft Defender cloud protection or sample submission is enabled
items.category: Defender cloud protection and sample submission
items.description: Disables Microsoft Defender cloud/MAPS reporting, automatic sample submission, Block at First Seen, and high cloud blocking levels.
items.severityReason: cloud protection and sample submission can send file metadata or suspicious files to Microsoft
items.threatProofExactQuote: scan it in the cloud
items.fixProofExactQuote: 0: Disabled. Send no information to Microsoft. This is the default value.
items.downsides: Microsoft Defender cloud-delivered protection, Block at First Seen, and automatic cloud analysis can become less effective.
checkCommands: Get-MpPreference | Select-Object MAPSReporting, SubmitSamplesConsent, DisableBlockAtFirstSeen, CloudBlockLevel | Format-List
items.applicabilityNote: applies where Microsoft Defender Antivirus and Set-MpPreference are available

items.name: Microsoft Defender MAPS or sample submission is not disabled by policy
items.category: Defender cloud protection and sample submission
items.description: Disables Microsoft Defender MAPS cloud reporting and file sample submission using machine policy registry values.
items.severityReason: Defender MAPS and sample submission can send cloud protection telemetry or file samples to Microsoft
items.threatProofExactQuote: You can disconnect from the Microsoft Antimalware Protection Service. | You can stop sending file samples back to Microsoft.
items.fixProofExactQuote: SpyNetReporting to 0 (zero) | SubmitSamplesConsent to 2 (two) for Never Send.
items.downsides: Microsoft Defender cloud-delivered protection and automatic sample analysis become less effective. Tamper Protection can prevent changing these policy-backed settings until it is turned off.
checkCommands: Get-ItemProperty -LiteralPath 'HKLM:\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows Defender\Spynet' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object SpyNetReporting, SubmitSamplesConsent
items.applicabilityNote: applies where Microsoft Defender Antivirus policy registry values are honored; Tamper Protection may block changes

items.name: Malicious Software Removal Tool reporting is not disabled
items.description: Disables Malicious Software Removal Tool diagnostic infection reporting to Microsoft.
items.severityReason: MSRT reporting can disclose malware detection and infection context from the device
items.threatProofExactQuote: You can turn off Malicious Software Reporting Tool (MSRT) diagnostic data
items.fixProofExactQuote: DontReportInfectionInformation to 1.
items.downsides: Microsoft receives less infection reporting from MSRT, which can reduce malware prevalence telemetry and related protection insights.
items.applicabilityNote: applies where the Malicious Software Removal Tool policy registry value is honored

items.name: Windows Search cloud sources are allowed
items.description: Prevents Windows Search and Cortana from searching cloud sources such as OneDrive and SharePoint.
items.severityReason: cloud search can expose account-connected content lookups and cloud-source query context
items.threatProofExactQuote: search cloud sources like OneDrive and SharePoint
items.fixProofExactQuote: 0 Not allowed.
items.downsides: Windows Search will stop showing cloud-backed results from OneDrive, SharePoint, or account-connected sources.
items.applicabilityNote: Policy CSP Search/AllowCloudSearch lists device scope for Enterprise, Education, and IoT Enterprise

items.name: Windows Search web results are allowed
items.description: Prevents Windows Search from performing web queries and showing web results.
items.severityReason: web search integration can send typed search terms and search context to web services
items.threatProofExactQuote: queries will be performed on the web and web results will be displayed
items.fixProofExactQuote: 0 Not allowed. Queries won't be performed on the web and web results won't be displayed when a user performs a query in Search.
items.downsides: Web results disappear from Windows Search; local file, app, and settings search remains available.
items.applicabilityNote: Policy CSP Search/DoNotUseWebResults is not listed for Pro

items.name: Windows Search can use location
items.description: Prevents Search and Cortana from using location for location-aware results.
items.severityReason: location-aware search can expose location context through search-related services
items.threatProofExactQuote: search and Cortana can access location information
items.fixProofExactQuote: The most restrictive value is `0` to not allow search to use location.
items.downsides: Location-aware Search and Cortana results stop working.
items.applicabilityNote: Policy CSP Search/AllowSearchToUseLocation lists device scope for Pro, Enterprise, Education, and IoT Enterprise

items.name: Cortana is allowed by Search policy
items.description: Disables Cortana through the Windows Search policy registry value.
items.severityReason: Cortana and search-linked assistant features can make Microsoft service connections and process query context
items.threatProofExactQuote: Allow Cortana Choose whether to let Cortana install and run on the device.
items.fixProofExactQuote: REG_DWORD: AllowCortana Value: 0
items.downsides: Cortana is disabled on Windows versions where this policy applies.
items.applicabilityNote: documented as Windows 10/11 restricted-traffic Group Policy/registry setting; Cortana availability varies by Windows version

items.name: Office client diagnostic data is not set to Neither
items.description: Sets Microsoft 365 Apps client software diagnostic data to Neither for the current user.
items.severityReason: Office client diagnostic data is sent to Microsoft unless this policy is set to Neither
items.threatProofExactQuote: Diagnostic data is used to keep Office secure and up-to-date
items.fixProofExactQuote: SendTelemetry 1=Required 2=Optional 3=Neither
items.downsides: Microsoft receives less Office diagnostic data, which can reduce troubleshooting and product-improvement telemetry.
items.applicabilityNote: applies when Microsoft 365 Apps for enterprise/business desktop apps are installed

items.name: Office connected experiences are not disabled
items.description: Disables most connected experiences in Microsoft 365 Apps for the current user.
items.severityReason: Office connected experiences can use Microsoft cloud services and online storage integrations
items.threatProofExactQuote: document stored on Microsoft OneDrive or translating the contents of a Word document
items.fixProofExactQuote: DisconnectedState 1=Enabled 2=Disabled
items.downsides: Coauthoring, online file storage integration, and many Office cloud-connected features become unavailable or degraded.
items.applicabilityNote: applies when Microsoft 365 Apps for enterprise/business desktop apps are installed

items.name: Office connected experiences that analyze content are not disabled
items.description: Disables Office connected experiences that analyze document content for the current user.
items.severityReason: Office content-analysis features can send document content or derived content context to Microsoft cloud services
items.threatProofExactQuote: experiences that use your Office content
items.fixProofExactQuote: UserContentDisabled 1=Enabled 2=Disabled
items.downsides: Designer, Translator, Smart Lookup-like features, and other content-analysis experiences become unavailable or degraded.
items.applicabilityNote: applies when Microsoft 365 Apps for enterprise/business desktop apps are installed

items.name: Office optional connected experiences are not disabled
items.description: Disables additional optional connected experiences in Microsoft 365 Apps for the current user.
items.severityReason: optional connected experiences are Microsoft-direct services and can process user content or workflow outside the organization agreement
items.threatProofExactQuote: offered by Microsoft directly to your users
items.fixProofExactQuote: ControllerConnectedServicesEnabled 1=Enabled 2=Disabled
items.downsides: Optional connected experiences such as some Bing-backed Office features become unavailable.
items.applicabilityNote: applies when Microsoft 365 Apps for enterprise/business desktop apps are installed

items.name: OneDrive file sync is not disabled by policy
items.description: Prevents apps and Windows features from working with OneDrive file storage and stops OneDrive file synchronization with the cloud.
items.severityReason: OneDrive is a first-party cloud file exfiltration path for user and application content
items.threatProofExactQuote: apps and features can work with OneDrive file storage
items.fixProofExactQuote: OneDrive files aren't kept in sync with the cloud.
items.downsides: OneDrive app integration, file picker integration, camera roll upload, and cloud file sync stop working.
items.applicabilityNote: Policy CSP System/DisableOneDriveFileSync lists device scope for Pro, Enterprise, Education, and IoT Enterprise; only relevant where OneDrive is present

items.name: OneDrive KFM, silent sign-in, personal sync and external sync are not blocked
items.category: OneDrive account and folder redirection hardening
items.description: Blocks Known Folder Move to OneDrive, prevents automatic silent sign-in, blocks syncing of personal OneDrive accounts, and prevents syncing libraries or folders shared from other organizations.
items.severityReason: Desktop/Documents/Pictures, user credentials, and externally shared cloud content can be synchronized automatically or with minimal user friction
items.threatProofExactQuote: Documents, Pictures, and Desktop folders to any OneDrive account
items.fixProofExactQuote: Block users from signing in with a Microsoft account
items.downsides: Known Folder Move protection, silent onboarding, personal OneDrive sync, and B2B external library sync stop working.
checkCommands: Get-ItemProperty -LiteralPath 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\OneDrive' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object KFMBlockOptIn, SilentAccountConfig, BlockExternalSync, DisableOfflineModeForExternalLibraries, KFMSilentOptIn, KFMOptInWithWizard
items.applicabilityNote: applies where OneDrive sync client policy registry keys are honored

items.name: Edge cloud synchronization is not disabled
items.description: Disables Microsoft Edge cloud synchronization through Microsoft sync services.
items.severityReason: Edge profile data can be synchronized through Microsoft sync services
items.threatProofExactQuote: cloud synchronization
items.fixProofExactQuote: Example registry value 0x00000001
items.downsides: Cross-device Edge sync stops working.
items.applicabilityNote: applies where Microsoft Edge policy support is available

items.name: Edge personalization reporting is not disabled
items.description: Disables Microsoft Edge personalization reporting that can send browsing history, favorites, collections, usage, and other browsing data to Microsoft.
items.severityReason: Edge personalization reporting can send browsing history, favorites, collections, usage, and other browsing data to Microsoft services
items.threatProofExactQuote: collecting a user's Microsoft Edge browsing history, favorites and collections, usage, and other browsing data
items.fixProofExactQuote: If you disable this policy, users can't change or override the setting.
items.downsides: Personalized ads, Microsoft Edge, search, news, and other Microsoft service personalization based on Edge browsing data will be disabled.
items.applicabilityNote: applies where Microsoft Edge policy support is available; policy applies to profiles signed in with a Microsoft account

items.name: Edge diagnostic data is not disabled
items.description: Disables required and optional Microsoft Edge diagnostic data about browser usage where the Edge policy is supported.
items.severityReason: Edge diagnostic data can include browser usage, visited websites, and crash reports sent to Microsoft
items.threatProofExactQuote: Optional diagnostic data includes data about how you use the browser, websites you visit, and crash reports to Microsoft
items.fixProofExactQuote: Off (0) = Off (Not recommended)
items.downsides: Microsoft Edge sends less diagnostic data, which can reduce browser troubleshooting, reliability, and product-improvement telemetry.
items.applicabilityNote: applies where Microsoft Edge policy support is available; Edge version 122 and later supports this policy on Windows

items.name: Windows browser settings sync group is not disabled
items.description: Disables the Windows 'browser' settings sync group so browser-related settings such as history and favorites are not synchronized through Windows settings sync.
items.severityReason: browser-related settings can roam across devices through Windows settings sync
items.threatProofExactQuote: history and favorites
items.fixProofExactQuote: won't be synced
items.downsides: Windows-level browser settings roaming stops. This does not replace Edge-specific sync hardening; use both.
items.applicabilityNote: Policy CSP Experience setting sync policies list device scope for Pro, Enterprise, Education, and IoT Enterprise

items.name: Optional diagnostic log collection is not limited
items.description: Prevents optional diagnostic logs from being collected when Windows needs more troubleshooting data.
items.severityReason: optional diagnostic logs can be sent to Microsoft when optional diagnostic data is enabled
items.threatProofExactQuote: Diagnostic logs are only sent when the device has been configured to send optional diagnostic data.
items.fixProofExactQuote: By enabling this policy setting, diagnostic logs won't be collected.
items.downsides: Some advanced troubleshooting workflows receive fewer diagnostic logs.
items.applicabilityNote: Policy CSP System/LimitDiagnosticLogCollection lists device scope for Pro, Enterprise, Education, and IoT Enterprise on Windows 11 21H2 and later; Home may honor the registry value but this is not officially listed

items.name: Optional diagnostic dump collection is not limited
items.description: Limits optional diagnostic dump collection to smaller dump types when Windows needs troubleshooting data.
items.severityReason: optional diagnostic dumps can include full or heap dumps and be sent to Microsoft
items.threatProofExactQuote: we may occasionally collect full or heap dumps if the user has opted to send optional diagnostic data.
items.fixProofExactQuote: Windows Error Reporting is limited to sending kernel mini dumps and user mode triage dumps.
items.downsides: Some advanced crash troubleshooting data becomes less detailed.
items.applicabilityNote: Policy CSP System/LimitDumpCollection lists device scope for Pro, Enterprise, Education, and IoT Enterprise on Windows 11 21H2 and later; Home may honor the registry value but this is not officially listed

items.name: Windows Search history or Bing web integration is enabled
items.description: Disables device search history, Bing web results in Windows Search, and storage or display of recent search-box suggestions while keeping local file, application, and settings search available.
items.severityReason: web search integration can send typed search terms to an external search provider, while device search history stores previous queries locally
items.threatProofExactQuote: Search can perform queries on the web
items.fixProofExactQuote: queries won't be performed on the web
items.downsides: Bing web results, web suggestions, and recent search-query suggestions will disappear. Local search for indexed files, installed applications, and Windows settings should remain available.
items.applicabilityNote: current-user Search settings are Windows-version dependent; optional policy value may require supported editions
